titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick J. Mallon to Herbet Henry Asquith, 29 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick J. Mallon

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from solicitor Patrick Joseph Mallon (1881-1974) to Herbert Henry Asquith (1852-1928) regarding the vacant clerkship of the Crown and Peace for Westmeath. Mallon is applying to Asquith as the position of lord lieutenant was temporarily vacant in the aftermath of the Easter Rising. Herbert Henry Asquith (1852-1928) was Liberal Prime Minister of the United Kingdom from 1908 to 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916-5616-11468

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Irish Office Coralana, Lissmacaffrey Co. Westmeath, 29 th June 1916 To the Right Hon. H. Asquith. M.P. Prime Minister Sir, I am a candidate for the vacant Clerkship of the
Crown & Peace for country Westmeath. I practised as
a Solicitor in that County and also Longford for past
twelve years. As the appointment rests with the
Lord Lieutenant who I understand has resigned,
I put in my application for the position to you.
I beg to refer you to Mr John P. Hayden. M.P.
Mullingar Co. Westmeath. for reference. I have
the honour to remain Sir
your obedient servant Patrick. J. Mallon I ask & state that
an appointment has been
made.
